question:
What will be the output of the following code? 

```python
set_a = {1, 2, 3}
set_b = {3, 4, 5}
result = set_a & set_b
print(result)
```
response:
Okay, so set_a has {1, 2, 3} and set_b has {3, 4, 5}. The code uses the & operator between them. I think & means intersection, but I'm not totally sure. Intersection would be the common elements, right? So only 3 is in both sets. 

But wait, maybe & is union? No, union would combine all elements. That would be {1,2,3,4,5}, which isn't an option. So probably intersection. 

My answer is: B. Because 3 is the only number in both sets.
